This game is basically:
''Try again'' simulator... but with bones flying in 4K

the game is very fun! i recommend it, and the skins are very easy to get/unlock, the easiest way is to sign up for WB Newsletter, claim the 1k krystals you got, and maybe buy a skin in the shop, but the real easiest one is to do character tutorials, they are very easy to do, and the gameplay is VERY fun, i cant get bored of it imo, its very high quality

MK11 is very very fun, its very easy to learn and you'll get used to it immediately, I play it on my keyboard but a lot of people say to play it on controller to be better or smth idk but its alright., cool story mode and I had lots of fun in online matches too

The online bug where you just cannot acces half the game is still a ting 6 years after they "patched it" and the only way to maybe get it to work is to either run windows 10 (still inconsistent) or pray you can sighn in one day regardless of how old your account is it does not matter.
devs dropped it, and the game is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ itself to death... no online, which means its basically unplayable... gg

You know , im trying to enjoy the game, not sweating on some A.I's that are just spamming combos and cant even defend myself . This makes the player use the AI Fighter and leave the game open which is a boring thing to do.

whoever designed the towers of time modifiers is an actual ♥♥♥♥. All the modifiers are just spam projectiles that interrupt your grabs and combos, dealing stupid amounts of damage. I had one against skarlet where the projectiles also healed her. It takes away any incentive to actually play the game properly because you just get spammed to death anyway, so in the end you just spam upper cuts in hopes of cheesing the fight. Best part is they lock majority of the customisation behind grinding this mode, which takes up most of your play time after beating the story. Like holy ♥♥♥♥ ed boon, I want to play the game not get juggled for twenty minutes by flying skulls that cross the entire screen faster than the speed of light. The game is ultimately fun when it allows you to actually engage with its mechanics, but if you want to ever customise your character you better get used to having a bad time.
Good game besides the spamming ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that will stay in the corner and wait for you to do anything and hit you with their special ability
The game is pretty fun. The story mode is pretty decent, gameplay is fun, and overall, a pretty good game.
graphics and game play is terrific new characters brings the excitement and suspense on another level
i mean this game is good but its hard to do some combos and some special moves and fatalities but ill try my best to play this game and train hard
amazing game, super fun Kombat, and really good story, little bit slower than mkx but good for casual players as well.

Mortal Kombat 11 is easily one of my favorite fighting games.
The graphics are absolutely insane, and the character animations are some of the best I’ve ever seen in the genre. Every punch, kick, and Fatality looks incredible and has so much impact.
The combat feels solid, responsive, and satisfying to learn. Whether you’re playing casually or trying to improve, there’s a lot to enjoy. On top of that, the game offers a huge amount of content, with plenty of characters, modes, cosmetics, and unlockables to keep you busy for a long time.
I honestly love this game more than I can express. It looks amazing, plays great, and always gives me a reason to come back for more.
Highly recommended.

Solid game. Wish a few classic characters made it into the game over some of the pop culture ones. Majority of them have some sore of firearm or blaster etc... so feels like pretty much like the same character with a reskin but very good game buy this over the latest MK game for sure.
this game is good has some problems with online servers but overall its good and story is interesting and good i will give it 8/10
If you like when the opponent runs away and point a gun at your face, this is the game for you.

Lowkey...When i first came back to this game after MK1 released i felt a nice sense of relief. Firstly this game is actually fun and isn't just middle aged men sweating d*ck just to beat my butt with omni fart. I would say this is one of if not the best MK game to date.

[b]Merits:[/b]
▪ Very good graphics
▪ Satisfying and cinematic story
▪ Good roster of characters
▪ Interesting location for the Krypt mode

[b]Flaws:[/b]
▪ Extremely grindy if you want to unlock stuff
▪ Limited number of skins, mostly palette swaps
▪ Time Krystal bug still present

[b]Verdict:[/b]
I had a great time playing this game. It is easy to pick up and hard to master. I especially enjoyed all the lore tid bits, being able to walk on Shang Tsung's Island and connect the locations was nice. However, I am disappointed about the skins and gears being locked behind payment or an extremely tedious grind. On top of that, if you buy the Ultimate Edition, it will show the Time Krystal packs as purchased, despite not actually granting them. This also locks you out of future purchases. If those things were not so, this game would be excellent. Still, I can recommend it, especially if you have friends to play with in local versus games.

[b]Rating:[/b]
★★★★☆ - Great
bro literally this is the best ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ mortal kombat game, mk mobile is alright but this is just, different, also dont run this on average pcs cuz this can ♥♥♥♥ up your pc

Mortal Kombat 11 is an absolute blast — easily one of the best fighting games I've ever played. The combat is smooth, satisfying, and endlessly replayable. I've been playing it with my sister and we can't stop laughing — especially because whoever loses has to deal with our younger brothers watching from the sidelines, ready to jump in and take their spot. It turns every match into a family event. If you enjoy competitive, fast-paced fighting games that are fun solo but even better with others around, this is a must-buy. Highly recommended!

Mortal Kombat 11 — это просто огонь. Одна из лучших файтинг-игр, в которые я когда-либо играл. Геймплей плавный, динамичный и не надоедает даже после многих часов. Мы играем вместе с сестрой, и это настоящий праздник — тот, кто проигрывает, сразу оказывается под пристальным взглядом наших младших братьев, которые уже сидят наготове и ждут своей очереди занять место. Каждый матч превращается в семейное соревнование! Если вы любите быстрые и зрелищные файтинги, которые веселее всего в компании, — берите не задумываясь. Однозначно рекомендую!

Purchased the ultimate edition few days ago,all I can say is that its worth every penny.Online multiplayer is still active,it has a good storyline,better guest characters than MK1,graphics still holds to this day.

Horrible story, gameplay very slow, a disgrace disguised as mortal kombat.
Takes time to get the hang of it but is really fun and love the graphics

If you find MK11 on sale today, grab it! The story is interesting, the roster is huge, and the fighting mechanics are arguably enjoyable. While I do love the X-ray, Krushing Blow added this certain crisp in doing kombos. Plus, it's visually appealing. The entire game is! In my opinion, the MK characters have never looked better than in this game, except probably for Sub-Zero's base design. That's why I love the range of customizations you can do with MK11, and the fair amount of DLC skins it offers (though it's a shame they never added 2021's MK and 2026's MKII movie skins). Also, the Krypt may be something you need to heavily grind for, even for certain fatalities, but there are guides and hacks that exist today on how you can get specific items much quicker. As a kasual gamer and a MK fan, I'd say MK11 is one of the best MK games despite its flaws, possibly even better than its successor, MK1. So, a 90% off it gives you more than what it's already worth.

That said, this game has some fun characters and combat. The Krypt is pretty cool (though some parts require extremely tedious grinding).

Even cheating it would probably take forever to get everything, 

but it is fun for a casual play or an extremely dedicated player of the series. Completionists who aren't dedicated MK players will suffer. 6/10. Get it on sale.

Pros:
Cinematic Story: Epic time-travel campaign with movie-quality production.
Strategic Combat: Slower, tactical gameplay focusing on spacing and mind-games.
Brutal Visuals: Stunning graphics, detailed character models, and jaw-dropping Fatalities.
Great Netcode: Smooth, lag-free online matches thanks to excellent rollback netcode.
Cons:
Heavy Grind: Unlocking skins and gear requires too much repetitive play.
Always Online: Single-player modes require a constant internet connection to save rewards.
The new designs can be a little dull. But the game itself is very entertaining
mkx has its charm but im one of those people who think mk11 just improved everything. the kombat feels much more smoother and zesty. the story is something else entirely with an unforgettable ending

Enough has been written about the fortnite-ification of Mortal Kombat that I'm not going to complain about it here. Yes this game is full of microtransactions and annoying guest characters who don't fit. But I'm gonna focus on the story and the gameplay. Please be aware I am a not a dedicated fighting game player and am merely a lifelong fan of the MK series for it's plot and world building. 

Story is good. I skipped this game back when it came out for dumb culture war brainrot reasons but now that I'm coming back to it with a fresh head it's really well done. Feels like a continuation of the trilogy rather than a story written soley for reboot purposes. So that's all I really care about. It made me appreciate characters I didn't like originally (Jade being one) and characters like Johnny continue to be awesome. I think it's great that such a goofball became such a good hero/father and this story really highlights that. One major problem comes from Sonya's stunt cast voice acting. Every line of dialogue she has makes me cringe inside out and for such a Sonya heavy plot that's a big deal. What's insane is I turned on the movie DLC skin and the voice acting was 10 million times better, just a shame you can't do that in story mode. The Kombat Kidz are much less prevalent and much less annoying than they were in X thank the lord. 

But the actual fighting is fun, not as fun as X at least for me. It's less about beatdowns and more about being careful and exchanging moves like it's an rpg. In that regard it actually shares more DNA with the original trilogy than the ps2 trilogy or 9 and X. I found it rewarding but hard to adjust to at first. 

The art style is more colorful than MKX which is a plus. But I think the character design suffered a lot, some of the costumes are horrendous and look like you're wandering around a convention hall not the wastes of outworld. 
Everyone has complained already that the default female character outfits are all various attempts to hide as much skin as possible, which is annoying yes but it's not like the male skins are any better. Erron Black is a specific example of this. At least the female skins you can set to be more traditionally pretty, I couldn't find any skins that made Erron Black go back to his MKX appearance, he always manages to look like roy rogers. The character model I think is the least flattering is Sheeva. I get that she's supposed to be a monster but c'mon, D'vorah's a monster, Mileena's a monster, and they still get to be cute. Poor Sheeva is stuck looking like Shrek.

So again that comes down to, it'd be nice to set costumes and skins for story mode but of course that's a tall order and I know it's not 2011 anymore and netherrealm has to worry about HR and twitter and all that stuff so I am sympathetic to the decisions they made, however I don't agree with them. I think MK should always be controversial and pushing the envelope like it was known for back in the day. But I feel as if it's slipped into just 'gore' and gore doesn't shock anyone anymore. Thankfully the storytelling makes up for this a lot for me. WITH ONE MAJOR EXCEPTION.

This game has possibly the most devastating assassination of a major character I have ever seen in a franchise. No I'm not talking about Mileena I guess she can be a lesbian if she wants, but I prefer shipping her with Baraka (don't judge me) now I'm talking about what this game does to Jax. Not in story mode, his ptsd and isolation is played straight and is very powerful. HOWEVER his tower ending, without wishing to spoil. Is one of the most insulting things anyone could possible do to his character and reads like someone just looked at his skin and was like 'oh this is what he'd do if he could time travel', not accounting for his personality, history, or any of three decades worth of character development.

I played this on steam deck and was able to a high resolution 60fps with most of the bells and whistles enables though it did make my machine feel a little toasty by the end of a session. The only exception seemed to be the Krypt which runs very poorly but I'm told that's true for more than just steam deck.

Overall though, I really regret not playing this when it came out as it is a high quality entry in the series obfuscated by dumb corporate decisions that get in the way of the creative product but do not ruin it in my opinion. You don't need to grind for skins or whatever to enjoy this game so all the live service BS can be safely ignored. Though I'd recommend the movie DLC and the classic costumes for purists like me.

Horrible game. Feamale characters look like trash. Offensive/defensive meter system is a joke and no longer rewards pressure. This game has more microtransactions than any f2p live service game out there. Because buying a WB game only gets you a limited access, rather than full experience. They even go as far as selling easy fatalities and then brag about accessibility the next minute. Towers of time is the worst thing to ever come out of MK games. You're fighting modifiers, not opponents. All of your skill and fundamentals go out the window, modifiers are broken with absolutely no counterplay, forcing you to take unavoidable damage or pretty much insta-kill you, there are hidden modifiers nearly every fight in adddition to the ones you already know of. Every fight becomes luck based rather than depend on skill

This game is good if we are talking about graphics,gore,gameplay (this has to be the Mk with the best gameplay) but it has bad things as well like that it's woke (more than the previous Mk),the female characters have more clothes,the bodies are "more realistic" and Mileena and Tanya are confirmed to be lesbians in this game.

As a Mortal Kombat franchise fan since MK9, this game brought me a lot of fun, not only overwhelming me with the graphic (even better than MK1 in my opinion), but also the playstyle, simple for new player. It is like a dream since 2019 when I first see the trailer, playing this game somehow made me achieve my childhood's goal in 2025 (sr for bad English:3 )
Optimization and frame rate are pretty good, and the story and characters are interesting.
The single player modes are not fun at all, it's just random modifier ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ that adds nothing but frustration to the gameplay.

The game looks good, the characters and the backgrounds. The game’s not as fast paced as Mortal Kombat X, and it’s more combo focused than the other one, but the combos feel pretty reasonable to execute for the most part.

If you just want the game for pvp, especially for playing against people in real life, you know, in person, then it’s good.

The only negative for this is the fatal combos. Essentially, when you get to 30% hp, you can use a fatal combo by pressing LT and RT. That’s it. You also get super armor while you perform the attack, and if it hits you do the full combo. The combo deals about half of their hp, or more.

I’ll reiterate, you get rewarded for doing badly. If you get low on health first, you can deal an absurd amount of damage with very little effort, and you can possibly kill the enemy with it, despite them being in the better position. It just feels idiotic.

I think you can only do it once successfully in a 2 out of 3, but if you miss you can just do it again later. It doesn’t even need bars or anything. Other than this, I’d say it’s still good as a pvp game or doing single battles against ai.

But the primary content is living towers, and it’s also the main way of unlocking things for all of the characters, it’s also the easiest way to get currency to unlock everything else in the krypt, so you’ll want to do it.

But they have consumables. These consumables can give you a huge advantage, assuming you have them from sort of just getting them randomly, because you do consume them, whether you beat the tower or you lost.

Whether or not you use consumables, the enemies use them in the living towers. This can be freezing you if you get close to them, if you don’t have a consumable to make you immune to freezing, or poison if you don’t have an item to make you completely immune to poison.

The main consumable you’ll see from the ai is a summon, where another character appears and attacks. Maybe a set of melee and a throw that allows the AI to attack you along with them, or a ranged ability, or a stun ability.

The only way to deal with this is to block it or try to dodge it. But it’s on a short cooldown. They can do it every few seconds and they spam it ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ constantly.

I’m not even really watching fights most of the time, I have to look at the box in the top right corner to see when it’s off cooldown so I can block or jump or whatever it is.

And sometimes, after blocking the attack from the summon and the ai, the next use of it is almost off cooldown, it’s that ridiculous.

If you win or you lose, it feels terrible, it’s not enjoyable. The living towers in mortal kombat X were enjoyable, these aren’t. It’s just a bunch of mobile gaming ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥.

Speaking of mobile gaming, they also have AI battles. They also let you use the ai to fight in towers for you, almost as if they knew how terrible the towers are, so they let you have the ai do it for you. But if the main gameplay is so terrible that you don’t want to do it, and you just want AI to unlock everything instead of actually playing, then what’s the point?

They also have consumables to let you skip fights and do easy fatalities. And no, summoning characters isn’t like a team battle, it’s just a consumable, but you can use it over and over again in a battle.

You’re not even open to a counter when you use it, and neither is the ai, so they just spam it as soon as it’s off cooldown, unless you’re already blocking and they know it’s actually blockable. Of course you just have to stop blocking and then block again, but it’s just bad game design.

In actual team battles, the teammate can be open to take damage, and you might have to actually do an action, but not this game. It just feels terrible to play against, and it even feels annoying to use your own consumables, because aside from feeling like you didn’t earn it, you know you’re wasting your consumables and you can’t easily just get more of it. The whole system is just extremely annoying.

It’s a great game with terrible mechanics holding it down and sort of ruining it, so I can’t really recommend it.

I recently got the game in a bundle, I've only played a little bit but I've been having a lot of fun (even tho I kinda suck at the game 😅). 
That being said, I think its best to have a decent spec PC for this (personally had a 4060 and the game ran extremely smoothly, tho I'd advise to check out a benchmark test on YouTube with your specs first), Since otherwise, u might not have the best time with it. since these games depend a lot on frame data.
As a beginner, Its simple but satisfying so far, the mechanics are not too complicated (tho I would advise using a guide if you are a complete beginner like me).
I've only played a few online matches, the matchmaking was good for me personally so far.
I have not played the story so no comment there
All in all, its very fun once u get the hang of it. Would recommend.
its a great game, i just feel like there are some bugs in the game but otherwise a very good game

Audio-Visuals and Performance: 10/10 (The graphics, motion capture, and finishing move animations are top-notch in the fighting game world)  
Story Mode: 9.0/10 (The ultimate popcorn blockbuster, the time-travel storyline is very engaging)  
Combat and Accessibility: 8.5/10 (Extremely friendly to beginners, one-button combos are great, but the strategic depth is still hardcore)  
Collection and Progression (Krypt): 4.0/10 (The first-person maze is extremely frustrating, grind-heavy and pay-to-win, heavily deducts points)  
Long-term Playability: 7.0/10 (Depends on your tolerance for frustration in online battles, single-player content runs out quickly)  

In 20 hours, I witnessed Liu Kang's epic reshaping of the universe, enjoyed the generational feud between Scorpion and Sub-Zero, and mastered how to slice opponents into sashimi using Baraka’s bone blades.  
*Mortal Kombat 11* is a game of “very long strengths and clear weaknesses.” It has some of the best visuals in fighting games and extremely beginner-friendly guidance, yet it includes a frustratingly cruel collection system that makes you want to smash the controller.  
If you are a ‘graphics fan,’ enjoy Hollywood action blockbusters, or just want a game to beat up friends offline, go for it—its visual feast is sure to impress. But if you are a ‘completionist’ player, or have low tolerance for being mercilessly beaten in online matches, then after finishing the story mode, it’s best to lock it away in your library.  
I just finally found a hidden chest in the Krypt, only to get a helmet with a 'common color,' which almost made me crush the controller. I’d better go online and find some bronze-level newbies to spar with to regain my confidence—I hope this time I won’t stay in the air for more than 10 seconds.
its like if you took out all the fun and satisfaction from mkx and mk1 then put in a progression system worse than modern warfare 3 2023

Mortal Kombat 11 features a good story that kept me interested throughout the campaign. Kronika was a challenging final boss, and I was only able to defeat her on the canonical way, which made the victory feel satisfying. The concept of different timelines and worlds colliding was interesting, and it felt like the developers may have taken some inspiration from MCU. The combat system was well designed and offered a balanced and enjoyable fighting experience. The graphics, powered by Unreal Engine, looked excellent and helped bring the characters and arenas to life. Among the guest characters, Spawn was my favorite addition, and I also spent a lot of time enjoying the multiplayer modes. Overall, Mortal Kombat 11 is a polished and highly entertaining fighting game.
